NYC Marathon · Running for Breast Cancer Research

Running
for Joy

In loving memory of Auntie Joy. ICU nurse, incredible aunt, and one of the best people I've ever known.

NYC Marathon

In Honor Of
Auntie Joy
Cause

Her story

She was pure Joy — in every sense of the word.

I’m running the New York City Marathon in memory of my Auntie Joy, who we lost to breast cancer in 2016. She lived just down the road from us, so she was part of our everyday lives. After school and on weekends, my sisters and I spent a lot of time at her house with her and my Nana.

Joy was an ICU nurse who spent her life caring for others. She would sew our halloween costumes, taught me how to knit, and made the best raspberry squares around holiday time.

She touched so many lives as a nurse, as an aunt, and as a friend. Losing her to breast cancer in 2016 left a hole in our family that has never closed. She deserved so much more time.

That's why I'm running.

The weight of it

Breast cancer touches so many of us.

1 in 8
Women in the US will be diagnosed in their lifetime
310K
New cases expected in the US this year
99%
Five-year survival rate when caught early. Research matters

Research is what moves that needle. Every dollar that goes into breast cancer science means earlier detection, better treatments, and more time for families to hold on to the people they love.

Life with Joy

A life well loved.

Make a difference

Help me run toward a cure

Every contribution funds research that brings us closer to a world where no one has to say goodbye too soon.
